authors: Kapural L, Yu C, Doust MW, Gliner BE, Vallejo R, Sitzman BT, Amirdelfan K, Morgan DM, Yearwood TL, Bundschu R, Yang T, Benyamin R, Burgher AH
title: Comparison of 10-kHz High-Frequency and Traditional Low-Frequency Spinal Cord Stimulation for Chronic Back and Leg Pain: 24-Month Results
study_question: Is HF10 superior to traditional low-frequency SCS for chronic back and leg pain at 24 months?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic intractable back and leg pain refractory to conservative therapy at 11 US centers.
outcome_measures: Responder rates, VAS back and leg pain, ODI, pain remission, patient satisfaction
inclusion_criteria: Chronic trunk or limb pain, refractory 3 months, back and leg VAS at least 5, ODI 41-80
answer_to_study_question: HF10 showed superior durable back and leg pain relief versus traditional SCS through 24 months.
reasoning: Strong agreement across models on bibliographic fields. I chose the title variant matching the 24-month paper. Participant count favored 198 by two high-confidence models; 171 likely reflects analyzed completers.
